sentence_reviews.text: Vectors, matrices, tensor contractions, eigensystems, and linear maps form the numerical language of modern machine learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Appendix A.2 'Vectors, Matrices, and Linear Algebra' (lines 768-774); related application chapters in Parts V-VI.
sentence_reviews.notes: The source directly supports vectors, matrices, and linear algebra as mathematical background, but it does not state the full machine-learning phrasing or the specific inclusion of tensor contractions and eigensystems. This is an atlas synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Linear algebra is to connect lower-level principles to downstream capabilities including Optimization theory, Numerical analysis, Dynamical systems & control theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Appendix A.2 (lines 768-774); Chapter 4 'Search in Complex Environments' / 4.1 'Local Search and Optimization Problems' (lines 103-110); Chapter 26.5 'Planning and Control' (lines 704-716).
sentence_reviews.notes: AIMA supports linear algebra as background and includes optimization and control topics, but it does not make the exact downstream-bridge claim in one place. 'Numerical analysis' is not directly supported by the cited source.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 4 'Search in Complex Environments' / 4.1 'Local Search and Optimization Problems' (lines 103-110); Appendix A.2 (lines 768-774).
sentence_reviews.notes: The source shows optimization as a major topic adjacent to mathematical background, but it does not explicitly say linear algebra is a substrate for optimization theory.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Appendix A.2 'Vectors, Matrices, and Linear Algebra' (lines 768-774).
sentence_reviews.notes: The source supports linear algebra as mathematical background, but it does not directly discuss numerical analysis. The example is plausible but the exact relationship is not established by the cited text.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Dynamical systems & control theory
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Appendix A.2 (lines 768-774); Chapter 26.5 'Planning and Control' and 26.5.3 'Trajectory tracking control' / 26.5.4 'Optimal control' (lines 704-716).
sentence_reviews.notes: The source clearly covers control topics and mathematical background, but it does not explicitly assert the substrate relation in this wording.

sentence_reviews.text: Its abstractions require explicit assumptions; a mathematically valid result can still be irrelevant to a mismatched empirical setting.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 27.1.3 'The mathematical objection' (lines 736-753); Appendix A.2 (lines 768-774).
sentence_reviews.notes: AIMA supports the general idea that mathematical formalisms have limits, but it does not directly state the empirical-mismatch caution in this exact form.
